## Type of Change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [ ] `feat` — New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] `fix` — Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] `docs` — Documentation only changes
- [ ] `style` — Code style / formatting (no functional change)
- [ ] `refactor` — Code refactor without new features or bug fixes
- [ ] `perf` — Performance improvement
- [ ] `test` — Adding or updating tests
- [ ] `chore` — Build process, dependency updates, tooling changes
- [ ] `ci` — Changes to CI/CD workflows
- [ ] `breaking` — Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project's coding conventions
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works
- [ ] Existing tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] I have updated the documentation where necessary
- [ ] I have updated `docs/changelog.md` with a summary of my changes
- [ ] My commit messages follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)
- [ ] I have checked for security implications (OWASP Top 10)
- [ ] I have not introduced any new dependencies without justification
